title: host-retarget output data
description:
The host-retarget outcome — the journaled login-node failover.

``stage_reached`` is ``host_retargeted``. ``old_cluster`` / ``new_cluster``
and ``old_ssh_target`` / ``new_ssh_target`` are the audit of what moved (the
run identity and its jobs did NOT move). ``decision_ts`` is the journaled
decision record's timestamp — the provenance trail finding 23 said the
hand-edit lacked.

stage_reached.description: The boundary reached — the cluster key was patched and journaled.
stage_reached.title: Stage Reached

run_id.description: The re-pointed run (its identity and jobs are unchanged).
run_id.title: Run Id

old_cluster.description: The run's cluster key before the failover.
old_cluster.title: Old Cluster

new_cluster.description: The run's cluster key after the failover.
new_cluster.title: New Cluster

old_ssh_target.description: The user@host the run was talked to through before.
old_ssh_target.title: Old Ssh Target

new_ssh_target.description: The user@host resolved from the new cluster key (now live for every consumer).
new_ssh_target.title: New Ssh Target

decision_ts.description: Timestamp of the journaled host-retarget decision (the provenance trail).
decision_ts.title: Decision Ts

reason.description: Human-readable one-line summary of the failover.
reason.title: Reason
